title: Kagurazaka Sightseeing Guide: Cobblestones, Shrines, and French Bistros
excerpt: A guide to Tokyo's 'Little Paris' — its temples, back alleys, and food scene — plus the spacious house rental right in the middle of it.
contentHtml:
Kagurazaka mixes old Edo-period charm with a distinctly French flavor, the legacy of a French school and community that settled here decades ago. The result is one of Tokyo's most atmospheric neighborhoods for an evening walk.

Top Sights

Kagurazaka's stone-paved alleys — Narrow, lantern-lit streets that feel like a different era, best explored after sunset.

Bishamonten Zenkokuji Temple — A neighborhood temple right on the main street, easy to combine with a walk through the area.

Akagi Shrine — A striking modern shrine redesign by architect Kengo Kuma.

Waseda University Campus — A short trip away, with a distinct academic atmosphere and historic buildings.

Food

Kagurazaka is known for its French restaurants tucked into converted machiya houses, alongside traditional Japanese kappo dining in the back alleys. Bakeries and cafes favored by the area's French residents add another layer worth exploring.

Where to Stay

For a group base right in the neighborhood, Plat House Kagurazaka is an 80㎡ house just 1 minute from the station, sleeping up to 10 guests with a full kitchen, Netflix, and Nintendo Switch. Direct booking beats OTA pricing, with free cancellation up to 7 days out.
